channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world

item.title: Grandmothers in Colombia get the quinceañera they never had
item.description: BOGOTÁ, Colombia (AP) — At age 72, Rosalba Casas finally celebrated her 15th birthday on Friday, donning a flowing pink dress and a tiara for the quinceañera she dreamed of as a teenager.
item.dc:creator: ASTRID SUÁREZ, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Russia faces a shrinking and aging population and tries restrictive laws to combat it
item.description: Russia has grappled with a shrinking and aging population for decades, with President Vladimir Putin viewing it as a threat to national security.
item.dc:creator: Katie Marie Davies, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Tropical Storm Melissa poised to become a hurricane and drop torrential rain on Caribbean
item.description: Tropical Storm Melissa is expected to strengthen into a hurricane, threatening the northern Caribbean with massive rainfall and life-threatening flooding.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Europe fights to avoid being sidelined by Trump’s world-shaping plans
item.description: Donald Trump has seized a central role on the global stage, leaving European allies on the sidelines.
item.dc:creator: Jill Lawless And Aamer Madhani, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Tropical Storm Melissa stationary in the Caribbean as 4 deaths reported and huge rains expected
item.description: SAN JUAN, Puerto Rico (AP) — Tropical Storm Melissa was nearly stationary in the central Caribbean on Friday as forecasters warned it could strengthen and swipe Jamaica as a powerful hurricane and dump a staggering amount of rain — up to 35 inches (89 centimeters) — on southwest Haiti, where they wa
item.dc:creator: DÁNICA COTO, JOHN MYERS JR. and EVENS SANON, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Trump’s Asia return and East Timor’s entry take spotlight in landmark ASEAN summit
item.description: KUALA LUMPUR, Malaysia (AP) — Southeast Asian foreign ministers opened talks Saturday ahead of a landmark ASEAN summit that will formally welcome East Timor as the bloc’s 11th member and mark U.S. President Donald Trump’s first trip to Asia since returning to the White House.
item.dc:creator: EILEEN NG, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Japan's new leader faces diplomatic gauntlet with Trump, China and regional summits
item.description: TOKYO (AP) — Just days after taking office, Japan's new leader faces a series of back-to-back foreign policy tests, with a meeting with U.S. President Donald Trump in Tokyo sandwiched between Asia-region summits in Malaysia and South Korea.
item.dc:creator: KEN MORITSUGU and MARI YAMAGUCHI, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Ukraine’s Zelenskyy urges US to broaden Russian oil sanctions and seeks long-range missiles
item.description: LONDON (AP) — Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelenskyy on Friday urged the United States to expand sanctions on Russian oil from two companies to the whole sector, and appealed for long-range missiles to hit back at Russia.
item.dc:creator: JILL LAWLESS, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Object that fell from sky in Australia is likely Chinese rocket debris
item.description: Officials from the Western Australia Police Force said miners in the Pilbara region reported discovering the blackened, smoldering mass Oct. 18.
item.dc:creator: Michael Ramsey, NewsNation

item.title: 48 bags of suspected human remains found at Mexican gravesite
item.description: Previous discoveries of bags found buried in Jalisco state gravesites have included bone fragments and human tissue.
item.dc:creator: Jeff Arnold, NewsNation

item.title: Frantic manhunt launched after asylum seeker who sexually assaulted teen accidentally freed from prison
item.description: An Ethiopian asylum seeker who sexually assaulted 14-year-old girl was accidentally released from a U.K. prison instead of being deported, sparking an urgent manhunt.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Trump hits Colombian President Petro with sanctions
item.description: The Trump administration leveled sanctions directly on Colombian President Gustavo Petro and his family members, escalating his feud with the South American leader.
item.dc:creator: Rebecca Beitsch, The Hill

item.title: Rubio tours US-led center in Israel overseeing the ceasefire in Gaza
item.description: KIRYAT GAT, Israel (AP) — U.S. Secretary of State Marco Rubio on Friday toured a U.S.-led center in Israel overseeing the Gaza ceasefire, as the Trump administration worked to set up an international security force in the territory and shore up the tenuous truce between Israel and Hamas.
item.dc:creator: MATTHEW LEE and WAFAA SHURAFA, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Afghanistan and Pakistan head to Turkey for second round of crisis talks
item.description: ISLAMABAD (AP) — Afghanistan and Pakistan are heading to Turkey for a second round of crisis talks, officials said Friday, after recent fighting between them killed dozens of people and injured hundreds more.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: US deploys Ford carrier strike group to combat narco-terror in Western Hemisphere
item.description: The Gerald R. Ford Carrier Strike Group is being deployed to continue targeting suspected drug smuggling boats in the Caribbean, the Pentagon said Friday.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Picasso portrait of muse Dora Maar in vivid hat, long hidden from view, sells for 32 million euros
item.description: A long-hidden Picasso portrait of muse Dora Maar has sold for 32 million euros at an auction in Paris.
item.dc:creator: Oleg Cetinic, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: International Criminal Court judges reject jurisdiction challenge in Duterte case
item.description: THE HAGUE, Netherlands (AP) — International Criminal Court judges on Thursday rejected a challenge to jurisdiction in the case of former Philippine President Rodrigo Duterte, who is accused of involvement in dozens of killings as part of his so-called war on drugs when in office.
item.dc:creator: MOLLY QUELL, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: EU leaders seek role in Gaza at summit focused on Ukraine and Russia
item.description: BRUSSELS (AP) — European Union leaders are seeking a more active role in Gaza and the occupied West Bank after being sidelined from the U.S.-brokered ceasefire between Israel and Hamas.
item.dc:creator: SAM McNEIL, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: PM Mark Carney says Canada will double its non-US exports
item.description: President Trump has been threatening Canada’s economy and sovereignty with tariffs, most offensively by claiming Canada could be “the 51st state.
item.dc:creator: ROB GILLIES, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Woman charged in $1.7M gold heist at Paris museum: Report
item.description: Investigators revealed the woman had left France the day of the break-in and was preparing to return to China.
item.dc:creator: Rob Taub, NewsNation

item.title: Putin directs drills of Russian nuclear forces as his summit with Trump is put on hold
item.description: MOSCOW (AP) — Russian President Vladimir Putin on Wednesday directed drills of the country’s strategic nuclear forces that featured practice missile launches, an exercise that came as his planned summit on Ukraine with U.S. President Donald Trump was put on hold.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Luigi Mangione brought up Brian Thompson's name while in Asia: Acquaintance
item.description: Christian Sacchini noted Luigi Mangione was shocked by how little an MRI scan cost in Thailand as opposed to the United States.
item.dc:creator: Rob Taub, NewsNation

item.title: American missionary kidnapped in Niger by suspected Islamist militants, sources say
item.description: Suspected Islamist militants have kidnapped an American missionary in the West African nation of Niger, U.S. official sources told Fox News.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: North Korea fires missiles as Trump prepares for South Korea visit
item.description: SEOUL, Oct 22 (Reuters) - North Korea fired what appeared to be multiple short-range ballistic missiles on Wednesday, South Korea's military said, a week before a key Asia-Pacific leaders' meeting in South Korea.
item.dc:creator: Jack Kim and Heejin Kim, NewsNation

item.title: Israel 'not a protectorate' of the US, Netanyahu says ahead of meeting with Vance
item.description: TEL AVIV, Israel (AP) — Israel's prime minister toughened his stance Wednesday by declaring that his country is in charge of its own security and isn't an American protectorate as he prepared to discuss progress on Gaza’s fragile ceasefire agreement with U.S. Vice President JD Vance.
item.dc:creator: MELANIE LIDMAN, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Trump pick for religious freedom envoy visits Holy Land, cites strong US-Israel bond
item.description: Baptist pastor Mark Walker discusses U.S.-Israel spiritual bond and plans to combat global religious persecution after Trump nomination as international religious freedom ambassador.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Massive Russian attack on Ukraine kills at least 6, officials say
item.description: Ukraine’s energy minister said a “massive combined overnight attack” on Ukraine’s energy infrastructure was still underway early Wednesday.
item.dc:creator: SUSIE BLANN, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Paris prosecutor says stolen Louvre jewels worth an estimated $102 million
item.description: PARIS (AP) — The Paris prosecutor said Tuesday that crown jewels stolen in a dramatic weekend Louvre heist were worth an estimated 88 million euros ($102 million), but that the monetary estimate doesn’t include their historical value to France.
item.dc:creator: SYLVIE CORBET, Associated Press, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Mexican lime growers' leader found dead after anti-cartel statements
item.description: In interviews, Bernardo Bravo ripped into organized crime's "permanent commercial hijacking of any commercial activity.
item.dc:creator: Anna Kutz, NewsNation

item.title: Sanae Takaichi becomes Japan’s first female prime minister, citing Thatcher inspiration
item.description: Sanae Takaichi becomes Japan's first female prime minister, citing Margaret Thatcher as inspiration while promising to restore confidence and strengthen security.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Ukraine and EU leaders accuse Putin of stalling, reject land concessions for peace
item.description: They accused Russian President Vladimir Putin of stalling for time in diplomatic efforts to bring his invasion of Ukraine to an end.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Virginia Giuffre, Epstein and Prince Andrew accuser, gets 'final word' in new memoir
item.description: The memoir serves as a discussion of surviving abuse, fighting for justice and suffering at the hands of Prince Andrew and disgraced financier Jeffrey Epstein.
item.dc:creator: Ashleigh Banfield, NewsNation

item.title: Dad sends 11-year-old to bed thinking he was drunk; boy later dies of snake bite
item.description: The cause of death for an 11-year-old boy in Australia was confirmed last week by the Coroners Court of Queensland.
item.dc:creator: Laura Morrison, NewsNation

item.title: Jane Goodall's cause of death revealed: Report
item.description: Epilepsy was also listed as one of Goodall's conditions, though it's not clear if it contributed to her death.
item.dc:creator: Michael Bartiromo, NewsNation

item.title: Pope meets with global group of clergy abuse survivors to talk zero tolerance
item.description: VATICAN CITY (AP) — Pope Leo XIV met Monday for the first time with an organization of clergy abuse survivors and advocates, who said he agreed to maintain a permanent dialogue as they press for a zero-tolerance policy for abuse in the Catholic Church.
item.dc:creator: NICOLE WINFIELD, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: China accuses US of cyberattack on national time center
item.description: China said any damage to related facilities could have disrupted network communications, financial systems and power supply.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Trump says Ukraine's Donbas region will have to be 'cut up' to end the Russian invasion
item.description: ABOARD AIR FORCE ONE (AP) — President Donald Trump said Sunday that the Donbas region of Ukraine should be “cut up," leaving most of it in Russian hands, to end a war that has dragged on for nearly four years. “Let it be cut the way it is,” he told reporters aboard Air Force One.
item.dc:creator: CHRIS MEGERIAN, Associated Press, WGN 9
